title: Script Redundancy Elimination
purpose: Eliminate duplicate script functionality, delete dead scripts, extract shared libraries, and ensure every script has a package.json entry or explicit dead declaration.
architectural_constraints: Dead scripts archived, not permanently deleted (90-day policy)
All remaining scripts have package.json entries
No silent deletions — emit governance events

deliverables.description: Shared stream-append logic extracted from emit-governance-event and emit-telemetry

acceptance_criteria: audit-runtime-integrity.ts archived (3 unique checks migrated to audit-runtime.ts)
emit-governance-event.ts and emit-telemetry.ts use shared stream-emitter.ts
diagnostics-health.ts merged into diagnostics-console.ts as --quick flag
bootstrap.ts writes to runtime/state/ (not legacy runtime-state.json)
self-heal.ts imports stale-lock detection from detect-stale-locks.ts
Dead scripts archived: generate-runtime-projections.ts, migrate-governance-md-to-json.ts, validate-authority.js, setup-test-db.ts
Incomplete validators either completed or removed
All remaining scripts have package.json entries

traceability.description: Eliminated script redundancy: extracted shared stream-emitter.ts, merged diagnostics-health into --quick flag, added 3 unique checks to audit-runtime.ts, archived 5 dead scripts, updated bootstrap.ts for runtime/state/ projections, integrated detect-stale-locks into self-heal.ts.

failure_modes.scenario: Deleted script has hidden caller discovered later
failure_modes.mitigation: 90-day archive before permanent deletion; grep all imports before action
